Installation of windom (and how to rebuild it)

Installation of windom

To get windom installed, you have to copy the headers files and the static library :
  • copy the content of the windom-distrib/include directory (including its windom subdirectory) with the headers files of your compiler (/usr/include, or /usr/m68k-atari-mint/include for example).
  • Then you should copy the library itself windom/lib/gcc/libwindom.a in the lib directory of your complier (/usr/lib for example).

If you use the Sparemint environment, then just install the RPM package.

Compiling

If you want to rebuild windom, it's higly recommanded to setup a complete sparemint environment because the building process needs a bash shell, gmake, sed, doxygen (for the documentation)... and a filesystem with long filenames.

You can also build the gcc version of windom by using a cross compiler under linux for example.
